title: Method and system for access to a secured building
abstract: The present invention provides for methods and systems for accessing a secured building. The method includes retrieving information from a code associated with a carrier and an address. The code may be a barcode with encoded information. The method further includes confirming that the address is associated with the secured building and further validating the carrier as an authorized carrier by an identification of the carrier. The method further includes validating a status associated with the information to be one of: in transit; and out for delivery. Upon positive response of the status, the method further includes allowing access to the secured building. In some embodiments, prior to allowing access to the secured building, the method further includes checking that an update to the status is within 24 hours.
claims: 1. A real-time method of accessing, by delivery personnel for delivery of a package to a locker in a locker system of a multi-tenant building or portion thereof (MTBOPT), the method comprising: obtaining, by an entry console of the MTBOPT, in accordance with a unique code associated with the package to be delivered, a tracking code associated with the package and a recipient address of the package to be delivered; forwarding, by the entry console, the tracking code and the recipient address to a building operating system (BOS); performing, by the BOS, a first verification procedure based on the tracking code and on the recipient address, the first verification procedure comprising: sending, by the BOS, a first verification request comprising the tracking code to one or more databases, the one or more databases comprising tracking information for a plurality of packages that include the package to be delivered, the tracking information including a delivery status for each of the plurality of packages and a delivery address for each of the plurality of packages; obtaining, by the BOS, in accordance with the one or more databases, the delivery status and the delivery address of the package to be delivered, the delivery status of the package to be delivered being out for delivery and the recipient address matching the delivery address; and determining, by the BOS, in accordance with the delivery status being out for delivery and the recipient address matching the delivery address, that access to the MTBOPT is permitted; updating, by the BOS, in accordance with access to the MTBOPT being permitted, the tracking information comprised in the one or more databases; and causing, by the BOS, the entry console to open a door of the MTBOPT; obtaining, by the locker system, a request to access the locker, wherein the request to access the locker comprises an access code associated with the package to be delivered; and forwarding, by the locker system, the access code to the BOS; performing, by the BOS, a second verification procedure based on the access code, the second verification procedure comprising: sending, by the BOS, a second verification request comprising the access code to the one or more databases; receiving, by the BOS, a time window associated with the package and during which the package to be delivered is to be deposited in the locker; comparing the access code in the second verification request to the tracking information in the one or more databases; and determining that the time window has expired or that the tracking information in the one or more databases indicates access to the MTBOPT is not granted; subsequent to determining that the time window has expired or that the tracking information in the one or more databases indicates access to the MTBOPT is not granted, obtaining, by the locker system, in accordance with the unique code associated with the package to be delivered, the tracking code associated with the package and the recipient address of the package to be delivered; and forwarding, by the locker system, the tracking code and the recipient address to the BOS; performing, by the BOS, a third verification procedure based on the tracking code and on the recipient address, the third verification procedure comprising: sending, by the BOS, another verification request comprising the tracking code to the one or more databases; obtaining, by the BOS, in accordance with the one or more databases, the delivery status and the delivery address of the package to be delivered, the delivery status being out for delivery and the delivery address matching the recipient address; and determining, by the BOS, in accordance with the delivery status being out for delivery and the delivery address matching the recipient address, that access to the locker is granted; and updating, by the BOS, the tracking information comprised in the one or more databases; and causing, by the BOS, the locker system to open a door of the locker.
